answer: ReNew Energy Global Plc announced plans to build a 6.5-GW solar ingot-wafer plant in Andhra Pradesh, investing ₹4,200 crore, aiming to localize supply chains and reduce import dependence. The facility is part of ReNew's ₹82,000 crore commitment to Andhra Pradesh's clean energy sector.

results.title: ReNew begins work on ₹4,200 crore solar ingot-wafer unit in Andhra Pradesh | Industry News - Business Standard
results.snippet: ## ReNew's 6.5 GW solar ingot-wafer facility in Andhra Pradesh aims to localise supply chains, cut import dependence and support the state's ambition to emerge as India's 'Silicon Coast'. Decarbonisation solutions provider ReNew Energy Global Plc (ReNew) on Thursday announced it was setting up a 6.5 Gigawatt (Gw) solar ingot-wafer manufacturing facility at Anakapalli district near Visakhapatnam in Andhra Pradesh. One of the first greenfield solar ingot-wafer manufacturing facilities in India, the Anakapalli project is part of ReNew’s planned commitment to invest ₹82,000 crore in Andhra Pradesh’s clean energy and manufacturing system, signed at the CII Partnership Summit 2025. Andhra Pradesh IT and Industries Minister Nara Lokesh pointed to a broader trend of companies such as Premier Energies, Websol Energy System and Waaree Energies choosing the state for advanced solar manufacturing, indicating the emergence of a full-stack ecosystem spanning modules, cells, wafers and ingots. ReNew is also setting up one of India’s largest hybrid renewable energy (RE) projects in Andhra Pradesh’s Anantapur district and will invest ₹22,000 crore ($2.4 billion).

results.title: ReNew Launches 6.5 GW Solar Ingot-Wafer Manufacturing Facility in Andhra Pradesh | energynews.pro

results.snippet:
# ReNew to invest ₹4,200 crore in 6.5 GW solar ingot-wafer facility in Andhra Pradesh.

The investment is part of the company’s broader ₹82,000 crore commitment in Andhra Pradesh announced earlier, aimed at accelerating clean energy and manufacturing growth in the state.
